title: Fix Maintenance Fund balance desynchronization between Dashboard and Maintenance section

requirements.text: Identify and eliminate duplicate or conflicting data sources for the Maintenance Fund balance. The Dashboard's FinancialMetrics component (showing €72,200) and the MaintenanceManagement component must both read from the exact same backend data source — the maintenance fund's computed balance derived from its transaction history — so both views always show identical, consistent values.

quotes: maintenance fund works on Maintenance section. on dashboard screen still says 72,000. i think our funds do not synchronize or we have duplicates

acceptanceCriteria: The Maintenance Fund balance shown on the Dashboard (FinancialMetrics/Financial Overview card) always matches exactly the balance shown in the Maintenance section (MaintenanceManagement component).
There is only one canonical source of truth for the Maintenance Fund balance — the backend actor's maintenance fund state or transaction-derived calculation.
No hardcoded or stale fallback values (e.g., 72,000 or 72,200) remain in any frontend component or hook for the Maintenance Fund balance.
After adding an inflow or expense in the Maintenance section, the Dashboard card also reflects the updated balance without requiring a full page reload.

requirements.text: Audit the backend Motoko actor to ensure the Maintenance Fund balance is computed from a single authoritative source (e.g., sum of inflow transactions minus outflow/expense transactions). Remove any separate balance variable that is updated independently and can diverge from the transaction-based calculation.

quotes: i think our funds do not synchronize or we have duplicates

acceptanceCriteria: The backend exposes a single query for the Maintenance Fund balance that is always derived from the same transaction log used by the Maintenance section.
No duplicate stable variables exist that track the Maintenance Fund balance separately from the transaction history.
The backend query used by FinancialMetrics/Dashboard returns the same value as the query used by MaintenanceManagement.

requirements.text: Ensure the React Query cache keys used by the Dashboard (FinancialMetrics and KPIWidgets components) and the Maintenance section (MaintenanceManagement component) for the Maintenance Fund balance either share the same query key or are properly invalidated together when any maintenance fund mutation (add inflow, add expense, reset) occurs.

quotes: on dashboard screen still says 72,000. i think our funds do not synchronize

acceptanceCriteria: Mutations in MaintenanceManagement (add inflow, add expense, reset) invalidate all related Dashboard query keys so the Dashboard card updates automatically.
The Dashboard Maintenance Fund card does not show a stale cached value after a transaction is recorded in the Maintenance section.
Both components call the same backend query endpoint for the Maintenance Fund balance.

constraints: Do not change the overall tab/page structure of the Dashboard or Maintenance section.
Do not remove or alter existing transaction history displayed in MaintenanceManagement.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file.
nonGoals: Adding new fund types or restructuring other funds (Main Fund, Investment Fund).
Changing the visual design or layout of the Dashboard cards.
Modifying authentication or access control logic.
